summary: Monster Shakedown Street jam and seamless second‑set flow
blurb: The show erupts with a monster Shakedown Street that launches into an unbroken second‑set chain of Samson, Gimme Some Lovin', He's Gone > Cryptical Envelopment, Drums > Space > The Other One and ends on a soaring Sugar Magnolia. Jerry’s solos are on fire, Brent’s synth work adds drama, and the rhythm section locks in tight throughout. Highlights also include Mississippi Half‑Step and Keep On Growing in the first set.
review: June 30, 1985 at Merriweather Post Pavilion is remembered as one of the band’s late‑summer high points. The first set rolls along with a bright “Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo,” a rollicking “C.C. Rider” and tight “Keep On Growing,” setting up a second set that detonates on an explosive Shakedown Street. From there the Dead ride a wave of momentum through Samson & Delilah, a gutsy Gimme Some Lovin', and a breathtaking transition from He’s Gone into Cryptical Envelopment that segues straight into a thunderous Drums > Space → The Other One suite before winding down with a soulful “Stella Blue,” a rollicking “Around & Around,” and an ecstatic Sugar Magnolia. Jerry Garcia is on fire, delivering soaring leads and vocal phrasing that lift the entire band; Brent Mydland’s synth‑mandolin textures add drama to the Shakedown climax while his vocals shine on Gimme Some Lovin'. Phil Lesh provides a deep, clean bass foundation that “blows woofers,” and the twin drummers drive every jam with precision. Bob Weir’s punchy rhythm guitar and occasional tremolo sparkle throughout. Across all sources the performance is described as “monster,” “tight,” and “legendary,” making this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead looking for that quintessential 1985 summer vibe.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Overwhelmingly positive sentiment across all recordings: multiple reviewers call the Shakedown Street jam "monster" and "epic," praise seamless segues (Cryptical > Drums > Space > Other One), and highlight Jerry's soaring solos, Brent's synth work, and tight rhythm section. No consistent negative performance comments appear.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with no dropouts, full set present and praised for capturing the legendary Shakedown Street jam in pristine detail.

key_highlights: Epic Shakedown Street that launches a nonstop second‑set marathon.
Seamless Cryptical Envelopment → Drums → Space → The Other One transition.
Brent Mydland’s dramatic synth/mandolin work on the Shakedown climax.
Jerry Garcia’s soaring solos and vocal phrasing throughout.

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring solos on Shakedown Street and Half‑Step, vocal phrasing praised; tight interplay with Brent, occasional rhythm slip on Samson but overall top form.
band_performance.Phil: Deep, clean bass that "blows woofers" during Space > Other One; strong foundation throughout and noted for powerful "bass bombs" in the second set.
band_performance.Bob: Thick, punchy guitar tone on Shakedown Street, tremolo work shines on opening jam, slide work described as pristine when heard.
band_performance.Brent: Dramatic synth/mandolin textures drive the Shakedown climax; cheesy 80s synth fun on Half‑Step and solid vocal contributions on Gimme Some Lovin' and Keep On Growing.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums > Space segment highlighted as "great"; his groove helps propel the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Billy: Driving tempo and tight groove noted throughout, especially during the Drums/Space transition.
